idea.hook: This planet experiences the wildest temperature swings in our solar system!
facts: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun, receiving intense solar radiation.
During its long day, surface temperatures can soar to 430°C (800°F), hot enough to melt lead.
But with almost no atmosphere to trap heat, night temperatures plummet to -180°C (-290°F).
This is a staggering daily temperature difference of over 600°C (1100°F)!
idea.payoff: Imagine a world where you'd be incinerated then frozen solid, all in one 'day'!
idea.title: Mercury: Daily Melt & Freeze! 🤯🌡️

scenes.text: Wildest Swings in the Solar System?
scenes.visual: A stylized view of Mercury appearing from darkness, with the Sun prominently in the background, emitting intense light.
A close-up shot of Mercury's intensely sunlit, rocky, cratered surface, shimmering with implied heat waves. The Sun is a bright, burning presence.
A rapid transition (like rotating the planet) to Mercury's dark, cratered night side, illuminated faintly by distant starlight, with visible frost or ice forming on the surface.

A split screen of Mercury: the left half is fiery red and molten-looking (day), the right half is icy blue and stark (night). A thermometer graphic animates in the center, showing the extreme temperature range.

scenes.text: INCINERATED THEN FROZEN... ALL IN ONE 'DAY'!
scenes.visual: A conceptual animation of a human silhouette rapidly changing from glowing red embers (incineration) to shattering ice (frozen solid), overlaid on a dramatic, fast-spinning Mercury backdrop.
